Kikuyu: Ningĩ ngũũria atĩrĩ: Ayahudi nĩkũhĩngwo maahĩngirwo makĩgũa ũndũ matangĩũkĩra? Kũroaga gũtuĩka ũguo! Aca ti guo o na atĩa! Ũrĩa kũrĩ nĩ atĩ, tondũ wa mehia mao, ũhonokio nĩũkinyĩire andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ nĩgeetha andũ a Isiraeli maigue ũiru.
English: I ask then, did they stumble that they might fall? May it never be! But by their fall salvation has come to the Gentiles, to provoke them to jealousy.

Kikuyu: No angĩkorwo ũremi wao nĩũtuĩkĩte ũtonga kũrĩ kĩrĩndĩ gĩa thĩ, nayo hathara yao ĩgatuĩka ũtonga kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ-rĩ, githĩ kũiyũrĩrĩrwo kwao gũtingĩkĩrehe ũtonga mũingĩ makĩria!
English: Now if their fall is the riches of the world, and their loss the riches of the Gentiles, how much more their fullness!

Kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, nĩ inyuĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ ndĩraarĩria. Tondũ ndĩ mũtũmwo ũtũmĩtwo kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ- rĩ, ũtungata ũcio wakwa nĩndĩwĩkĩrĩire mũno,
English: For I speak to you who are Gentiles. Since then as I am an apostle to Gentiles, I glorify my ministry,

Kikuyu: ngĩĩgeragĩria kana hihi ndahota kwarahũra andũ a rũruka rwakwa kĩũmbe maigue ũiru, na ndũme amwe ao mahonokio.
English: if by any means I may provoke to jealousy those who are my flesh, and may save some of them.

Kikuyu: Tondũ angĩkorwo kũregwo kwao nĩkuo gwa gũtũma kĩrĩndĩ gĩa thĩ kĩiguithanio na Ngai-rĩ, ĩ gwĩtĩkĩrwo kwao kũngĩkĩrehe kĩĩ, tiga o kũriũka kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ?
English: For if the rejection of them is the reconciling of the world, what would their acceptance be, but life from the dead?

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo kĩenyũ gĩa kĩmere kĩrĩa kĩrutĩirwo Ngai kĩrĩ maciaro ma mbere nĩ gĩtheru-rĩ, o nakĩo kĩmere gĩothe no gĩtheru; angĩkorwo mũri nĩ mũtheru-rĩ, o nacio honge no theru.
English: If the first fruit is holy, so is the lump. If the root is holy, so are the branches.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo honge imwe cia mũtamaiyũ wa mũgũnda nĩciakahũrirwo, nawe, o na watuĩka ũrĩ rũhonge rwa mũtamaiyũ wa gĩthaka-rĩ, ũgĩciarithanio na honge icio ingĩ, na rĩu nĩũgayanaga ũnoru na icio ingĩ kuuma mũri wa mũtamaiyũ ũcio-rĩ,
English: But if some of the branches were broken off, and you, being a wild olive, were grafted in among them and became partaker with them of the root and of the richness of the olive tree,

Kikuyu: menya ndũkae kwĩganĩra honge icio ingĩ. Ũngĩka ũguo-rĩ, ririkana ũndũ ũyũ: Wee tiwe ũnyiitĩrĩire mũri ũcio, no nĩ mũri ũcio ũkũnyiitĩrĩire.
English: don’t boast over the branches. But if you boast, remember that it is not you who support the root, but the root supports you.

Kikuyu: No wee no ũkiuge atĩrĩ, “Honge icio ciakahũrirwo nĩgeetha njiarithanio na mũtĩ.”
English: You will say then, “Branches were broken off, that I might be grafted in.”

Kikuyu: Ũguo noguo. No rĩrĩ, honge icio ciakahũrirwo tondũ wa kwaga gwĩtĩkia, nawe wĩhaandĩte nĩ ũndũ wa gwĩtĩkia. Ndũkae gwĩtĩĩa, no wĩtigagĩre.
English: True; by their unbelief they were broken off, and you stand by your faith. Don’t be conceited, but fear;

Kikuyu: Tondũ angĩkorwo Ngai ndaacaĩire honge icio cia ndũire-rĩ, o nawe ndangĩgũcaĩra.
English: for if God didn’t spare the natural branches, neither will he spare you.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mwĩcũraniei ũhoro wa ũtugi wa Ngai na kwaga tha gwake: harĩ andũ acio maagũire, nĩagire kũmaiguĩra tha, no inyuĩ, nĩkũmũtuga amũtugaga, angĩkorwo nĩmũgũtũũra thĩinĩ wa ũtugi wake. Kwaga ũguo-rĩ, o na inyuĩ no mũtinio mweherio.
English: See then the goodness and severity of God. Toward those who fell, severity; but toward you, goodness, if you continue in his goodness; otherwise you also will be cut off.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio nao mangĩtiga kũrũmia ũhoro ũcio wa kwaga gwĩtĩkia-rĩ, nĩmagaciarithanio na mũtĩ, nĩgũkorwo Ngai arĩ na ũhoti wa kũmaciarithania naguo rĩngĩ.
English: They also, if they don’t continue in their unbelief, will be grafted in, for God is able to graft them in again.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, angĩkorwo mwatinirio kuuma mũtamaiyũ-inĩ wa kĩrĩti, na mũgĩcooka mũgĩciarithanio na mũtamaiyũ wa mũgũnda, na ũcio nĩ ũhoro ũtarĩ wa ndũire-rĩ, githĩ ti ũhũthũ makĩria honge ici, o ici ciarĩ ciaguo, gũciarithanio na mũtamaiyũ wa mũgũnda o ũcio warĩ wao kĩũmbe!
English: For if you were cut out of that which is by nature a wild olive tree, and were grafted contrary to nature into a good olive tree, how much more will these, which are the natural branches, be grafted into their own olive tree?

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ndikwenda mũikare mũrigĩtwo nĩ ũhoro ũyũ wa hitho, nĩgeetha mũtikae kwĩona ta mũrĩ oogĩ: Hitho nĩ atĩ, andũ amwe a Isiraeli nĩanyiite nĩ kũũmia ngoro, o nginya rĩrĩa mũigana wa andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ ũkaahinga gũtoonya.
English: For I don’t desire you to be ignorant, brothers,† of this mystery, so that you won’t be wise in your own conceits, that a partial hardening has happened to Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, andũ a Isiraeli othe nĩmakahonokio, o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Mũhonokia nĩakoima Zayuni; naguo ũhoro wa kũregana na Ngai nĩakaweheria kuuma kũrĩ Jakubu.
English: and so all Israel will be saved. Even as it is written, “There will come out of Zion the Deliverer, and he will turn away ungodliness from Jacob.

Kikuyu: Nakĩo gĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa ngaarĩkanĩra nao, rĩrĩa ngaamehereria mehia mao.”
English: This is my covenant with them, when I will take away their sins.”*

Kikuyu: Ha Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega-rĩ, andũ a Isiraeli nĩmatuirwo thũ nĩ ũndũ wanyu, no ha ũhoro wa gũthuurwo-rĩ, nĩmendetwo nĩ ũndũ wa maithe mao ma tene,
English: Concerning the Good News, they are enemies for your sake. But concerning the election, they are beloved for the fathers’ sake.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo iheo iria Ngai aheanaga na gwĩtana kũrĩa etanaga nakuo ndacookaga kwĩricũkwo.
English: For the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa inyuĩ hĩndĩ ĩmwe mwaremeire Ngai, no rĩu nĩamũiguĩrĩire tha nĩ ũndũ wa ũremi wao-rĩ,
English: For as you in time past were disobedient to God, but now have obtained mercy by their disobedience,

Kikuyu: rĩu no taguo o nao matuĩkĩte aremi, nĩgeetha o nao rĩu maiguĩrwo tha nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa Ngai amũiguĩrĩire tha.
English: even so these also have now been disobedient, that by the mercy shown to you they may also obtain mercy.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩatuĩte andũ othe aremi nĩgeetha acooke amaiguĩre tha othe.
English: For God has bound all to disobedience, that he might have mercy on all.

Kikuyu: Hĩ! Ĩ ũũgĩ wa Ngai, o naguo ũmenyo wake, itikĩrĩ nyingĩ na ndiku mũno! Matuĩro make gũtirĩ ũngĩhota kũmatuĩria, na mĩthiĩre yake kaĩ ndĩngĩmenyeka-ĩ!
English: Oh the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and the knowledge of God! How unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past tracing out!

Kikuyu: “Nũũ ũmenyete meciiria ma Mwathani? Kana nũũ ũkoretwo akĩmũtaara?”
English: “For who has known the mind of the Lord? Or who has been his counselor?”*

Kikuyu: “Nũũ wanahe Ngai kĩndũ, atĩ nĩguo Ngai acooke amũrĩhe kĩndũ kĩu?”
English: “Or who has first given to him, and it will be repaid to him again?”*

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo indo ciothe cioimire harĩ we, na nĩwe ũcitũũragia ciothe, na ciothe-rĩ, no ciake. Nake arogoocagwo nginya tene na tene! Ameni.
English: For of him and through him and to him are all things. To him be the glory for ever! Amen.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, ndamũthaitha tondũ wa ũrĩa Ngai anamũiguĩra tha, mwĩrute mĩĩrĩ yanyu ĩtuĩke magongona marĩ muoyo, na matheru mangĩkenia Ngai, arĩ guo ũtungata ũrĩa wagĩrĩire na wa ma wa kũgoocithia Ngai.
English: Therefore I urge you, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable to God, which is your spiritual service.

Kikuyu: Mũtikanahaananie ũhoro wanyu na maũndũ marĩa mekagwo nĩ andũ a thĩ ĩno; no garũrũkai na ũndũ wa kwerũhio meciiria manyu. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũrĩhotaga kũmenya na gwĩtĩkĩra wendi ũcio wa Ngai, o wendi ũcio wake mwega ũrĩa wa kũmũkenagia, na mũkinyanĩru kũna.
English: Don’t be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what is the good, well-pleasing, and perfect will of God.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa wega ũrĩa ndaaheirwo nĩ Ngai-rĩ, ngwĩra o mũndũ wothe wanyu atĩrĩ: Mũndũ ndakae gwĩtua mũnene gũkĩra ũrĩa aagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩtua, no nĩ kaba mũndũ eciiragie ũhoro wake we mwene na atue itua rĩagĩrĩru, kũringana na wĩtĩkio ũrĩa Ngai amũheete.
English: For I say through the grace that was given me, to everyone who is among you, not to think of yourself more highly than you ought to think; but to think reasonably, as God has apportioned to each person a measure of faith.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa tũrĩ na ciĩga nyingĩ thĩinĩ wa mwĩrĩ ũmwe, nacio ciĩga ici ciothe itirutaga wĩra mũthemba ũmwe-rĩ,
English: For even as we have many members in one body, and all the members don’t have the same function,

Kikuyu: no taguo o na ithuĩ, o na twatuĩka tũrĩ aingĩ, tũrĩ mwĩrĩ ũmwe thĩinĩ wa Kristũ, na o kĩĩga nĩkĩohanĩtio na iria ingĩ ciothe.
English: so we, who are many, are one body in Christ, and individually members of one another,

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩtũheetwo iheo itiganĩte, kũringana na wega ũrĩa tũheetwo. Angĩkorwo kĩheo kĩa mũndũ nĩ kũratha mohoro-rĩ, nĩahũthĩre kĩheo kĩu kũringana na ũrĩa wĩtĩkio wake ũigana.
English: having gifts differing according to the grace that was given to us: if prophecy, let’s prophesy according to the proportion of our faith;

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo nĩ gũtungata, nĩatungatage; na angĩkorwo nĩ kũrutana, nĩarutanage;
English: or service, let’s give ourselves to service; or he who teaches, to his teaching;

Kikuyu: ningĩ angĩkorwo nĩ kũũmĩrĩria andũ, nĩakĩũmanagĩrĩrie; na angĩkorwo nĩ wa kũrũmbũiya mabata ma andũ arĩa angĩ, nĩakĩheanage na ũtaana; angĩkorwo nĩ wa gũtongoria, nĩatongoragie na kĩyo; ningĩ ũrĩa wa kũiguanĩra tha, nĩekage ũguo akenete.
English: or he who exhorts, to his exhorting; he who gives, let him do it with generosity; he who rules, with diligence; he who shows mercy, with cheerfulness.

Kikuyu: Wendani wanyu nĩũkoragwo nĩ ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũhinga. Thũũragai ũndũ ũrĩa mũũru; naguo ũndũ ũrĩa mwega mũkĩũrũmagie.
English: Let love be without hypocrisy. Abhor that which is evil. Cling to that which is good.

Kikuyu: Endanagai mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ, mũrĩ na wendani ta wa ciana cia ithe ũmwe. Na mũndũ nĩatĩĩage ũrĩa ũngĩ gũkĩra we mwene.
English: In love of the brothers be tenderly affectionate to one another; in honor prefer one another,

Kikuyu: Rutagai wĩra na kĩyo, na mũreke ngoro cianyu ihĩahĩage, mũgĩtungatagĩra Mwathani.
English: not lagging in diligence, fervent in spirit, serving the Lord,

Kikuyu: Ikaragai mũkenete nĩ ũndũ mũrĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro, na mũkiragĩrĩrie hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna, na mũikarage mũkĩhooyaga hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: rejoicing in hope, enduring in troubles, continuing steadfastly in prayer,

Kikuyu: Teithagiai andũ a Ngai mabataro-inĩ mao. Tũũrai mũnyiitaga andũ ũgeni.
English: contributing to the needs of the saints, and given to hospitality.

Kikuyu: Rathimagai andũ arĩa mamũnyariiraga; marathimagei na mũtikanamarume.
English: Bless those who persecute you; bless, and don’t curse.

Kikuyu: Kenagai hamwe na arĩa marakena; na mũcakae na arĩa maracakaya.
English: Rejoice with those who rejoice. Weep with those who weep.

Kikuyu: Tũũrai mũiguanĩte mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ. Tigagai gwĩtĩĩa, no mwĩtĩkagĩre kũgĩa ngwatanĩro na andũ arĩa matarĩ igweta. Tigagai kwĩona ta mũrĩ oogĩ.
English: Be of the same mind one toward another. Don’t set your mind on high things, but associate with the humble. Don’t be wise in your own conceits.

Kikuyu: Mũtikaneke mũndũ ũũru nĩ ũndũ wa ũũru ũrĩa amwĩkĩte. Menyagĩrĩrai nĩguo mwĩkage maũndũ marĩa mangĩonwo magĩrĩire nĩ andũ othe.
English: Repay no one evil for evil. Respect what is honorable in the sight of all men.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ-rĩ, geragiai gũikarania na mũndũ o wothe na thayũ kũngĩhoteka.
English: If it is possible, as much as it is up to you, be at peace with all men.

Kikuyu: Arata akwa, mũtikanerĩhĩrie, no tigagĩriai mangʼũrĩ ma Ngai handũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Kũrĩhanĩria nĩ gwakwa; nĩ niĩ ngaarĩhanĩria,” ũguo nĩguo Mwathani ekuuga.
English: Don’t seek revenge yourselves, beloved, but give place to God’s wrath. For it is written, “Vengeance belongs to me; I will repay, says the Lord.”*

Kikuyu: Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ: “Thũ yaku ĩngĩkorwo ĩhũtiĩ-rĩ, mĩhe irio; ĩngĩkorwo nĩĩnyootiĩ-rĩ, mĩhe gĩa kũnyua. Gwĩka ũguo-rĩ, nĩkũmĩigĩrĩra makara ma mwaki mũtwe.”
English: Therefore “If your enemy is hungry, feed him. If he is thirsty, give him a drink; for in doing so, you will heap coals of fire on his head.”*

Kikuyu: Mũtikanatoorio nĩ ũũru, no tooragiai ũũru na wega.
English: Don’t be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

Kikuyu: O mũndũ no nginya athĩkagĩre arĩa maathanaga, nĩgũkorwo gũtirĩ hinya wa gwathana tiga ũrĩa Ngai ahaandĩte. Aathani arĩa marĩ ho nĩ Ngai ũmatuĩte a gwathana.
English: Let every soul be in subjection to the higher authorities, for there is no authority except from God, and those who exist are ordained by God.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo-rĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa ũremagĩra arĩa maathanaga, nĩkũrega aregaga ũndũ ũrĩa Ngai atuĩte, nao arĩa mekaga ũguo nĩmagatuĩka a gũtuĩrwo ciira.
English: Therefore he who resists the authority withstands the ordinance of God; and those who withstand will receive to themselves judgment.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo aathani ti a kũmakia arĩa mekaga ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire, no nĩ a kũmakia arĩa mekaga ũũru. Nĩũkwenda wagage gwĩtigagĩra ũrĩa wathanaga? Gĩĩkage ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire, nake nĩarĩkũgathagĩrĩria.
English: For rulers are not a terror to the good work, but to the evil. Do you desire to have no fear of the authority? Do that which is good, and you will have praise from the authority,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo we nĩ ndungata ya Ngai ya gũtũma wonage maũndũ mega. No ũngĩka ũũru-rĩ, wĩtigĩre, nĩgũkorwo ndakuuaga rũhiũ rwa njora tũhũ. We nĩ ndungata ya Ngai, na mũrĩhania wake wa kũherithania na wa gũkinyĩria mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga ũũru mangʼũrĩ ma Ngai.
English: for he is a servant of God to you for good. But if you do that which is evil, be afraid, for he doesn’t bear the sword in vain; for he is a servant of God, an avenger for wrath to him who does evil.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, nĩ kwagĩrĩire mwathĩkagĩre arĩa maathanaga, ti tondũ wa gwĩtigĩra mangʼũrĩ macio, no nĩ tondũ wa ũrĩa mwĩmenyeire na thamiri cianyu ũrĩa arĩ wega gwĩka.
English: Therefore you need to be in subjection, not only because of the wrath, but also for conscience’ sake.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio noguo ũtũmaga mũrutage magooti, nĩgũkorwo arĩa maathanaga nĩ ndungata cia Ngai, na maheanaga mahinda mao mothe nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra ũcio kĩũmbe.
English: For this reason you also pay taxes, for they are servants of God’s service, continually doing this very thing.

Kikuyu: Rutagĩrai andũ othe kĩrĩa mwagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũmarutĩra: Angĩkorwo mũtirutĩte magooti, rutai magooti; na angĩkorwo nĩ kũruta mbeeca cia kũrutithia wĩra wa bũrũri, rutai; nao andũ arĩa a kũheo gĩtĩĩo, maheagei; na arĩa a gwĩkĩrĩrwo, mekagĩrĩrei.
English: Therefore give everyone what you owe: if you owe taxes, pay taxes; if customs, then customs; if respect, then respect; if honor, then honor.

Kikuyu: Mũtigaikare mũrĩ na thiirĩ na mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ, tiga o mwendanage hĩndĩ ciothe mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ, nĩgũkorwo mũndũ wendaga mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ nĩahingĩtie watho.
English: Owe no one anything, except to love one another; for he who loves his neighbor has fulfilled the law.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ maathani maya, atĩ “Ndũkanatharanie,” na “Ndũkanoragane,” na “Ndũkanaiye”, na “Ndũkanacumĩkĩre kĩndũ kĩene,” o na rĩathani rĩngĩ o rĩothe rĩngĩkorwo ho, mohanĩtio mothe maturanĩirwo thĩinĩ wa rĩathani rĩĩrĩ: “Enda mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ ta ũrĩa wĩyendete wee mwene.”
English: For the commandments, “You shall not commit adultery,” “You shall not murder,” “You shall not steal,”† “You shall not covet,”* and whatever other commandments there are, are all summed up in this saying, namely, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”*

Kikuyu: Wendani-rĩ, ndũngĩĩka mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ ũũru. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio kwendana nĩkuo kũhingia watho.
English: Love doesn’t harm a neighbor. Love therefore is the fulfillment of the law.

Kikuyu: Na mwĩkage ũguo nĩ ũndũ wa kũmenya mahinda maya tũrĩ ũrĩa matariĩ. Ihinda nĩikinyu nĩgeetha mwarahũke mũtige gũkoma, tondũ ũhonokio witũ rĩu ũrĩ hakuhĩ kũrĩ rĩrĩa twambĩrĩirie gwĩtĩkia.
English: Do this, knowing the time, that it is already time for you to awaken out of sleep, for salvation is now nearer to us than when we first believed.

Kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũrĩ hakuhĩ gũthira; naguo mũthenya ũkiriĩ gũkinya. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩtũtigane na maũndũ ma nduma na twĩohe indo cia mbaara, o iria cia ũtheri.
English: The night is far gone, and the day is near. Let’s therefore throw off the deeds of darkness, and let’s put on the armor of light.

Kikuyu: Nĩtũthiiage na mĩthiĩre mĩagĩrĩru, o ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire gũikarwo nayo mũthenya, tũtigatoonye maruga-inĩ mooru marĩ na nyĩmbo njũru na ũrĩĩu, na tũtigatoonye ũtharia-inĩ kana maũndũ ma ũũra-thoni, na tũtikagĩage na njũgitano na kũiguanĩra ũiru.
English: Let’s walk properly, as in the day; not in reveling and drunkenness, not in sexual promiscuity and lustful acts, and not in strife and jealousy.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mwĩhumbei Mwathani Jesũ Kristũ, na mũtigatũũre mwĩciiragia o ũrĩa mũngĩhingia merirĩria ma mĩĩrĩ ĩno ya mehia.
English: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, for its lusts.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa ũkoragwo arĩ na wĩtĩkio mũhũthũ mwamũkĩrei, mũtekũmũtuĩra ciira nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa mataiguanĩirwo.
English: Now accept one who is weak in faith, but not for disputes over opinions.

Kikuyu: Wĩtĩkio wa mũndũ ũmwe-rĩ, nĩũmwĩtĩkĩrĩtie kũrĩa irio cia mĩthemba yothe, no mũndũ ũngĩ, ũrĩa ũrĩ wĩtĩkio mũhũthũ, arĩĩaga o mĩthemba ya nyeni.
English: One man has faith to eat all things, but he who is weak eats only vegetables.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩĩaga mĩthemba yothe ya irio ndakae kũmena mũndũ ũrĩa ũtekaga ũguo, nake mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩĩaga indo imwe-rĩ, ndakae gũtuĩra ciira mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩĩaga ciothe tondũ Ngai nĩamwĩtĩkĩrĩtie.
English: Don’t let him who eats despise him who doesn’t eat. Don’t let him who doesn’t eat judge him who eats, for God has accepted him.

Kikuyu: Wee ũkĩrĩ ũ nĩguo ũtuĩre ndungata ya mũndũ ũngĩ ciira? Mwathi wayo nĩwe ũmenyaga ĩrũngiĩ wega kana ĩgwĩte. Nayo nĩĩrĩrũgamaga, tondũ Mwathani nĩwe ũrĩ na ũhoti wa gũtũma ĩrũgame.
English: Who are you who judge another’s servant? To his own lord he stands or falls. Yes, he will be made to stand, for God has power to make him stand.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũmwe-rĩ, onaga ta kũrĩ mũthenya ũmwe mũtheru kũrĩ ĩrĩa ĩngĩ; nake mũndũ ũngĩ akoona mĩthenya yothe no ũndũ ũmwe. O mũndũ nĩemenyere wega na ngoro yake.
English: One man esteems one day as more important. Another esteems every day alike. Let each man be fully assured in his own mind.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wonaga mũthenya ũmwe ũrĩ wa mwanya-rĩ, ekaga ũguo nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani. Nake ũrĩa ũrĩĩaga nyama-rĩ, arĩĩaga nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani, nĩgũkorwo nĩ Ngai acookagĩria ngaatho; nake ũrĩa ũtacirĩĩaga-rĩ, ekaga ũguo nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani, na nĩ Ngai acookagĩria ngaatho.
English: He who observes the day, observes it to the Lord; and he who does not observe the day, to the Lord he does not observe it. He who eats, eats to the Lord, for he gives God thanks. He who doesn’t eat, to the Lord he doesn’t eat, and gives God thanks.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo gũtirĩ o na ũmwe witũ ũtũũraga nĩ ũndũ wake mwene, na gũtirĩ o na ũmwe witũ ũkuaga nĩ ũndũ wake mwene.
English: For none of us lives to himself, and none dies to himself.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ tũngĩtũũra muoyo-rĩ, tũtũũraga nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani; na tũngĩkua-rĩ, twakua nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio tũngĩkorwo tũrĩ muoyo kana tũkuĩte-rĩ, tũrĩ o a Mwathani.
English: For if we live, we live to the Lord. Or if we die, we die to the Lord. If therefore we live or die, we are the Lord’s.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũyũ nĩguo watũmire Kristũ akue na acooke ariũke, nĩguo atuĩke Mwathani wa arĩa akuũ na arĩa marĩ muoyo.
English: For to this end Christ died, rose, and lived again, that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.

Kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, ũgũtuĩra mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe wanyu ciira nĩkĩ? Kana ũkũmena mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa ithe wanyu nĩkĩ? Nĩgũkorwo ithuothe nĩtũkarũgama mbere ya gĩtĩ gĩa ciira kĩa Ngai.
English: But you, why do you judge your brother? Or you again, why do you despise your brother? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of Christ.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “ ‘Ti-itherũ, o ta ũrĩa niĩ ndũũraga muoyo, iru o rĩothe nĩrĩgaturio mbere yakwa; naruo rũrĩmĩ o ruothe nĩrũkoimbũrĩra Ngai,’ ũguo nĩguo Mwathani ekuuga.”
English: For it is written, “ ‘As I live,’ says the Lord, ‘to me every knee will bow. Every tongue will confess to God.’ ”*

Kikuyu: Hakĩrĩ ũguo-rĩ, o ũmwe witũ nĩakaheana ũhoro wake mwene mbere ya Ngai.
English: So then each one of us will give account of himself to God.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩtũgĩtige gũtuanĩra ciira ithuĩ ene. Handũ ha ũguo-rĩ, kaba mũtue na meciiria manyu atĩ mũtigatũma mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa ithe wanyu ahĩngwo kana agwe njĩra-inĩ.
English: Therefore let’s not judge one another any more, but judge this rather, that no man put a stumbling block in his brother’s way, or an occasion for falling.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩmenyete wega biũ ndĩ thĩinĩ wa Mwathani Jesũ, atĩ gũtirĩ irio irĩ thaahu cio nyene. No rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa wonaga kĩndũ ta kĩrĩ thaahu-rĩ, ũcio harĩ we kĩndũ kĩu nĩ kĩrĩ thaahu.
English: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean of itself; except that to him who considers anything to be unclean, to him it is unclean.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa thoguo nĩekũigua ũũru tondũ wa kĩrĩa ũrĩĩaga-rĩ, wee nĩũtigĩte gũthiĩ na mĩthiĩre ya wendani. Tiga gũtũma mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe wanyu ũcio Kristũ aakuĩrĩire oore nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩa ũrarĩa.
English: Yet if because of food your brother is grieved, you walk no longer in love. Don’t destroy with your food him for whom Christ died.

Kikuyu: Mũtikanareke ũndũ ũrĩa muonaga ũrĩ mwega ũtuĩke wa gũcambio.
English: Then don’t let your good be slandered,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki wa Ngai ti ũhoro wa kũrĩa na kũnyua, no nĩ wa ũthingu, na thayũ o na gĩkeno iria ciumaga kũrĩ Roho Mũtheru,
English: for God’s Kingdom is not eating and drinking, but righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.

Kikuyu: tondũ mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũtungatagĩra Kristũ na njĩra icio nĩakenagia Ngai, o na akoonagwo arĩ mwega nĩ andũ.
English: For he who serves Christ in these things is acceptable to God and approved by men.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, nĩtwĩkĩragei kĩyo gĩa gwĩkaga maũndũ marĩa marehaga ũhoro wa thayũ na magatũma tuonganĩrĩre hinya.
English: So then, let’s follow after things which make for peace, and things by which we may build one another up.

Kikuyu: Mũtikanathũkie wĩra wa Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa irio. Irio ciothe nĩ theru, no rĩrĩ, nĩ ũũru mũndũ kũrĩa kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩrĩa kĩngĩhĩtithia mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ.
English: Don’t overthrow God’s work for food’s sake. All things indeed are clean, however it is evil for that man who creates a stumbling block by eating.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba kwaga kũrĩa nyama, kana kũnyua ndibei o na kana gwĩka ũndũ ũngĩ o na ũrĩkũ ũrĩa ũngĩtũma mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa Ithe wanyu ahĩtithio.
English: It is good to not eat meat, drink wine, nor do anything by which your brother stumbles, is offended, or is made weak.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, ũndũ ũrĩa wĩtĩkĩtie ũkoniĩ maũndũ macio-rĩ, nĩũmenyeke gatagatĩ gaku na Ngai. Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũndũ angĩĩcuukĩra nĩ ũndũ wa kĩndũ kĩrĩa onaga kĩrĩ kĩega agĩĩka.
English: Do you have faith? Have it to yourself before God. Happy is he who doesn’t judge himself in that which he approves.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ angĩkorwo arĩ na nganja, acooke arĩe irio iria arĩ na nganja nacio, mũndũ ũcio nĩetuĩrĩire ciira, tondũ kũrĩa gwake gũtiumanĩte na wĩtĩkio; naguo ũndũ o wothe ũtoimanĩte na wĩtĩkio nĩ mehia.
English: But he who doubts is condemned if he eats, because it isn’t of faith; and whatever is not of faith is sin. 24 Now to him who is able to establish you according to my Good News and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery which has been kept secret through long ages, 25 but now is revealed, and by the Scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the eternal God, is made known for obedience of faith to all the nations; 26 to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever! Amen.†

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ithuĩ arĩa tũrĩ na hinya-rĩ, twagĩrĩirwo nĩgũkuuanagĩra na arĩa matarĩ na hinya maũndũ mao ma ũhinyaru na to gwĩkenagia ithuĩ ene.
English: Now we who are strong ought to bear the weaknesses of the weak, and not to please ourselves.

Kikuyu: O ũmwe witũ nĩagĩrĩirwo nĩgũkenia mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ make mega, nĩgeetha ongererwo hinya.
English: Let each one of us please his neighbor for that which is good, to be building him up.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o na Kristũ ndekenirie we mwene, no o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Irumi cia arĩa makũrumaga nĩ niĩ ciagwĩrĩire.”
English: For even Christ didn’t please himself. But, as it is written, “The reproaches of those who reproached you fell on me.”*

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo maũndũ mothe marĩa maandĩkirwo tene maandĩkirwo marĩ ma gũtũruta, nĩgeetha tũgĩage na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro nĩ ũndũ wa gũkirĩrĩria na kũũmĩrĩrio nĩ Maandĩko macio.
English: For whatever things were written before were written for our learning, that through perseverance and through encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.

Kikuyu: Ngai ũrĩa ũheanaga ũkirĩrĩria na ũũmanĩrĩria aromũhe roho wa ũiguano o mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ rĩrĩa mũkũrũmĩrĩra Kristũ Jesũ,
English: Now the God of perseverance and of encouragement grant you to be of the same mind with one another according to Christ Jesus,

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha mũgoocithagie Ngai o we Ithe wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ mũrĩ na ngoro ĩmwe na mũgambo ũmwe.
English: that with one accord you may with one mouth glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, o mũndũ nĩagĩĩtĩkagĩre ũrĩa ũngĩ, o ta ũrĩa mwetĩkĩrirwo nĩ Kristũ, nĩgeetha Ngai agoocagwo.
English: Therefore accept one another, even as Christ also accepted you,† to the glory of God.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, Kristũ okire arĩ ndungata ya Ayahudi nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoro wa ma wa Ngai, nĩguo ahingie ciĩranĩro iria cierĩirwo maithe maitũ ma tene,
English: Now I say that Christ has been made a servant of the circumcision for the truth of God, that he might confirm the promises given to the fathers,

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ magoocithagie Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa tha ciake, ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngũkũgooca ndĩ gatagatĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ; nĩndĩrĩinaga nyĩmbo cia kũgooca rĩĩtwa rĩaku.”
English: and that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, “Therefore I will give praise to you among the Gentiles and sing to your name.”*

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩ kwĩrĩtwo atĩrĩ: “Kenai, inyuĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, mũrĩ hamwe na andũ ake.”
English: Again he says, “Rejoice, you Gentiles, with his people.”*

Kikuyu: Na ningĩ nĩ kwĩrĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Goocai Mwathani, inyuĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ othe, na mũmũinĩre nyĩmbo cia kũmũgooca, inyuĩ andũ othe.”
English: Again, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles! Let all the peoples praise him.”*

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ, Isaia nĩoigĩte atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtagwo Mũri wa Jesii nĩagooka, o we ũrĩa ũkaarahũka atuĩke wa gwatha ndũrĩrĩ, nao andũ-acio-a-Ndũrĩrĩ nĩmakaagĩa na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro thĩinĩ wake.”
English: Again, Isaiah says, “There will be the root of Jesse, he who arises to rule over the Gentiles; in him the Gentiles will hope.”*

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Ngai ũrĩa arĩ we mwĩhoko witũ aromũiyũria na gĩkeno gĩothe na thayũ tondũ wa ũrĩa mũmwĩhokete, nĩgeetha mũkĩrĩrĩrie kũgĩa na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa Roho Mũtheru.
English: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that you may abound in hope in the power of the Holy Spirit.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, niĩ mwene nĩnjĩtĩkĩrĩte atĩ inyuĩ ene nĩmũiyũrĩtwo nĩ wega, na nĩ mũrĩ na ũmenyo mũiganu, o na mũkahota gũtaarana inyuĩ ene.
English: I myself am also persuaded about you, my brothers,‡ that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish others.

Kikuyu: Ndĩmwandĩkĩire ũndũ ũyũ ndĩ na ũũmĩrĩru mũingĩ ngĩmwĩra maũndũ mamwe, taarĩ kũmũririkania maũndũ macio rĩngĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa wega ũrĩa Ngai aaheire,
English: But I write the more boldly to you in part as reminding you, because of the grace that was given to me by God,

Kikuyu: nĩguo nduĩke mũtungatĩri wa Kristũ Jesũ kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ, ndutage wĩra wa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai wa kũhunjagia Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Ngai, nĩgeetha andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ magaatuĩka igongona rĩĩtĩkĩrĩku nĩ Ngai, o rĩrĩa rĩtherie nĩ Roho Mũtheru.
English: that I should be a servant of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, serving as a priest of the Good News of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be made acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngwĩraha thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ nĩ ũndũ wa ũtungata wakwa ngĩtungatĩra Ngai.
English: I have therefore my boasting in Christ Jesus in things pertaining to God.

Kikuyu: Ndikũgeria kwaria ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ tiga o ũrĩa Kristũ ahingĩtie na ũndũ wakwa harĩ ũhoro wa gũtongoria andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ ũhoro-inĩ wa gwathĩkĩra Ngai, nĩ ũndũ wakwa kũheana ũhoro na maũndũ marĩa mekirwo,
English: For I will not dare to speak of any things except those which Christ worked through me for the obedience of the Gentiles, by word and deed,

Kikuyu: na ũndũ wa hinya ũrĩa watũmaga kũringwo ciama na kuonwo morirũ, tondũ wa hinya wa Roho Mũtheru. Na nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo kuuma Jerusalemu kũrigiicũka nginya Iluriko, nĩhunjĩtie biũ Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega wa Kristũ.
English: in the power of signs and wonders, in the power of God’s Spirit; so that from Jerusalem and around as far as to Illyricum, I have fully preached the Good News of Christ;

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ndũire ndĩĩrirĩirie kũhunjia Ũhoro-ũrĩa- Mwega kũrĩa Kristũ atamenyekete, nĩgeetha ndikanatuĩke wa gwaka igũrũ rĩa mũthingi wakĩtwo nĩ mũndũ ũngĩ.
English: yes, making it my aim to preach the Good News, not where Christ was already named, that I might not build on another’s foundation.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩgũtuĩke o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo, atĩrĩ: “Arĩa matarĩ meerwo ũhoro wake nĩo makaawona, na arĩa matarĩ maigua nĩo magaataũkĩrwo nĩguo.”
English: But, as it is written, “They will see, to whom no news of him came. They who haven’t heard will understand.”*

Kikuyu: Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩtũmi kĩrĩa kĩanagiria mahinda maingĩ njũke kũu kwanyu.
English: Therefore also I was hindered these many times from coming to you,

Kikuyu: Norĩu, tondũhatirĩhandũhangĩingĩrutawĩrangʼongo-inĩici, natondũmĩakamĩingĩnĩngoretwo ngĩĩrirĩria kũmuona-rĩ,
English: but now, no longer having any place in these regions, and having these many years a longing to come to you,

Kikuyu: nĩndĩrabanga gwĩka ũguo ngĩthiĩ Sipania. Nĩndĩrehoka gũkaamũceerera ngĩhĩtũkĩra kũu kwanyu, na atĩ nĩmũkandeithĩrĩria gũthiĩ rũgendo rũu, thuutha wa gũkenanĩra na inyuĩ ihinda inini.
English: whenever I travel to Spain, I will come to you. For I hope to see you on my journey, and to be helped on my way there by you, if first I may enjoy your company for a while.

Kikuyu: No rĩu ndĩ rũgendo-inĩ ngĩthiĩ Jerusalemu ngatungatĩre andũ arĩa aamũre marĩ kũu.
English: But now, I say, I am going to Jerusalem, serving the saints.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ a Makedonia na a Akaia nĩmoonire arĩ wega kũhotha nĩguo mateithie andũ arĩa athĩĩni a thiritũ ya andũ arĩa aamũre kũu Jerusalemu.
English: For it has been the good pleasure of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor among the saints who are at Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo moonire kwagĩrĩire, na ti-itherũ nĩ marĩ na thiirĩ wa andũ acio. Tondũ wa rĩĩrĩ, angĩkorwo andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ nĩmagwatanĩire na Ayahudi irathimo cia kĩĩroho-rĩ, o nao marĩ na thiirĩ kũrĩ Ayahudi wa kũmatungatĩra na indo iria marathimĩtwo nacio.
English: Yes, it has been their good pleasure, and they are their debtors. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, they owe it to them also to serve them in material things.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndaarĩkia kũruta wĩra ũcio, na menye wega atĩ maciaro macio nĩ marĩkia kwamũkĩrwo-rĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngamũceerera ngĩthiĩ Sipania.
English: When therefore I have accomplished this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will go on by way of you to Spain.

Kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ ngĩũkĩra kũu kwanyu, ngaakorwo njiyũrĩrĩirwo nĩ irathimo cia Kristũ.
English: I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of the Good News of Christ.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩndamũthaitha nĩ ũndũ wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ na nĩ ũndũ wa wendani ũrĩa tũheetwo nĩ Roho, atĩ mũnyiitanĩre na niĩ kĩgiano-inĩ gĩakwa na ũndũ wa kũhooya Ngai nĩ ũndũ wakwa.
English: Now I beg you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, that you strive together with me in your prayers to God for me,

Kikuyu: Hooyai nĩgeetha ngaahonokio kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa matetĩkĩtie a kũu Judea, na ningĩ atĩ ũtungata wakwa kũu Jerusalemu ũgetĩkĩrĩka nĩ andũ arĩa aamũre akuo,
English: that I may be delivered from those who are disobedient in Judea, and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha ngooka kũrĩ inyuĩ ngenete, Ngai enda, na tũcanjamũke tũrĩ hamwe.
English: that I may come to you in joy through the will of God, and together with you, find rest.

Kikuyu: Ngai mwene thayũ aroikara hamwe na inyuĩ inyuothe. Ameni.
English: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũgaathĩrĩria mwarĩ wa Ithe witũ Fibi kũrĩ inyuĩ, ũrĩa ũtungataga thĩinĩ wa kanitha wa Kenikirea.
English: I commend to you Phoebe, our sister, who is a servant† of the assembly that is at Cenchreae,

Kikuyu: Nĩngũmũhooya mũmwamũkĩre thĩinĩ wa Mwathani o ta ũrĩa mũngĩamũkĩra andũ arĩa aamũre, na mũmũhe ũteithio o wothe angĩbatario nĩguo kuuma kũrĩ inyuĩ, nĩgũkorwo o nake anakorwo arĩ ũteithio mũnene kũrĩ andũ aingĩ, o hamwe na niĩ mwene.
English: that you receive her in the Lord in a way worthy of the saints, and that you assist her in whatever matter she may need from you, for she herself also has been a helper of many, and of my own self.

Kikuyu: Geithiai Pirisila na Akula, arĩa tũrutaga wĩra nao tũrĩ thĩinĩ wa Kristũ Jesũ.*
English: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,

Kikuyu: O nĩmendire kũũragwo nĩ ũndũ wakwa. Na to niĩ nyiki ndĩramacookeria ngaatho, no nĩmacookeirio ngaatho nĩ makanitha mothe ma andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ.
English: who risked their own necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the Gentiles.

Kikuyu: Geithiai o na kanitha ũrĩa ũcemanagia kwao mũciĩ. Geithiai mũrata wakwa Epeneto, ũrĩa warĩ mwĩtĩkia wa Kristũ wa mbere kũu bũrũri wa Asia.
English: Greet the assembly that is in their house. Greet Epaenetus, my beloved, who is the first fruits of Achaia to Christ.

Kikuyu: Geithiai Mariamu, ũrĩa warutire wĩra na kĩyo kĩnene mũno nĩ ũndũ wanyu.
English: Greet Mary, who labored much for us.

Kikuyu: Geithiai Anderoniko na Juniasi, andũ a mũhĩrĩga witũ arĩa tuohetwo hamwe. Nao nĩ marĩ igweta gatagatĩ-inĩ ka atũmwo, o na ningĩ nĩmatuĩkire a Kristũ mbere yakwa.
English: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners, who are notable among the apostles, who were also in Christ before me.

Kikuyu: Geithiai Ampiliato, ũrĩa nyendete mũno thĩinĩ wa Mwathani.
English: Greet Amplias, my beloved in the Lord.

Kikuyu: Na mũgeithie Urubano, ũrĩa tũrutithanagia wĩra wa Kristũ nake, o na mũgeithie mũrata wakwa nyendete Sitakusi.
English: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and Stachys, my beloved.

Kikuyu: Geithiai Apele, ũrĩa wageririo na akĩmenyeka atĩ nĩ wa Kristũ kũna. Na mũgeithie andũ a nyũmba ya Arisitobulo.
English: Greet Apelles, the approved in Christ. Greet those who are of the household of Aristobulus.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ mũgeithie Herodioni, ũrĩa wa mũhĩrĩga witũ. Geithiai andũ a nyũmba ya Narikiso arĩa marĩ thĩinĩ wa Mwathani.
English: Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet them of the household of Narcissus, who are in the Lord.

Kikuyu: Geithiai Turufena na Turufosa, andũ-a-nja acio marutaga wĩra na hinya thĩinĩ wa Mwathani. Na mũgeithie mũrata wakwa Perisi, mũndũ-wa-nja ũngĩ o nake ũrutĩte wĩra na hinya mũno thĩinĩ wa Mwathani.
English: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labor in the Lord. Greet Persis, the beloved, who labored much in the Lord.

Kikuyu: Geithiai Rufusi, ũrĩa mũthuure thĩinĩ wa Mwathani, o na mũgeithie nyina, ũrĩa ũkoretwo arĩ o ta maitũ.
English: Greet Rufus, the chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ mũgeithie Asunikirito, na Felegoni, na Herime, na Pateroba, na Herima, o na ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ arĩa marĩ hamwe nao.
English: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers‡ who are with them.

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ mũgeithie Filologo, na Julia, na Nerea na mwarĩ wa nyina, na Olumpa, na arĩa othe aamũre makoragwo hamwe nao.
English: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.

Kikuyu: Geithaniai mũndũ na ũrĩa ũngĩ na ngeithi theru cia kĩmumunyano. Makanitha mothe ma Kristũ nĩmamũgeithia.
English: Greet one another with a holy kiss. The assemblies of Christ greet you.

Kikuyu: Ariũ na aarĩ a Ithe witũ, nĩngũmũthaitha mwĩmenyagĩrĩre andũ arĩa marehaga nyamũkano na maũndũ ma kũmũhĩngithia, marĩa mareganĩte na ũhoro ũrĩa mũrutĩtwo. Eheragĩrai andũ ta acio.
English: Now I beg you, brothers, look out for those who are causing the divisions and occasions of stumbling, contrary to the doctrine which you learned, and turn away from them.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ ta acio ti Mwathani witũ Kristũ matungataga, no nĩ nda ciao ene matungataga. Nĩmaheenagia ngoro cia andũ arĩa matooĩ ũũru na ũndũ wa mĩario yao mĩnyoroku na ndeto ciao cia kwĩyendithĩrĩria.
English: For those who are such don’t serve our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by their smooth and flattering speech they deceive the hearts of the innocent.

Kikuyu: Andũ othe nĩmaiguĩte ngumo ya wathĩki wanyu, nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩnjiyũrĩtwo nĩ gĩkeno nĩ ũndũ wanyu; no nĩngwenda mũgĩe na ũũgĩ harĩ maũndũ marĩa mega, na mwage ũcuuke harĩ maũndũ marĩa mooru.
English: For your obedience has become known to all. I rejoice therefore over you. But I desire to have you wise in that which is good, but innocent in that which is evil.

Kikuyu: Ngai mwene thayũ nĩarĩhehenja Shaitani o narua, na inyuĩ mũmũrangĩrĩrie rungu rwa magũrũ manyu. Wega wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ ũroikara na inyuĩ.
English: And the God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

Kikuyu: Nĩmwageithio nĩ Timotheo, ũrĩa tũrutaga wĩra nake, o na Lukio, na Jasoni na Sosipatero, arĩa a mũhĩrĩga witũ.
English: Timothy, my fellow worker, greets you, as do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.

Kikuyu: Niĩ Teritio, mwandĩki wa marũa maya, nĩndamũgeithia thĩinĩ wa Mwathani.
English: I, Tertius, who write the letter, greet you in the Lord.

Kikuyu: Nake Gayo, ũrĩa ũnyiitĩte ũgeni na ũrĩa ũtugaga andũ a kanitha othe, nĩamũgeithia. Nĩmwageithio nĩ Erasito, ũrĩa mũigi kĩgĩĩna gĩa itũũra, na mũrũ wa ithe witũ Kwarito. (
English: Gaius, my host and host of the whole assembly, greets you. Erastus, the treasurer of the city, greets you, as does Quartus, the brother.

Kikuyu: Wega wa Mwathani witũ Jesũ Kristũ ũrogĩa na inyuĩ inyuothe. Ameni.)
English: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all! Amen.

Kikuyu: Na rĩu kũrĩ ũrĩa ũngĩhota kũmũrũgamia wega nĩ ũndũ wa Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega ũrĩa hunjagia, na kwanĩrĩra ũhoro wa Jesũ Kristũ kũringana na ũguũrio wa maũndũ marĩa ma hitho marĩa matũire mahithĩtwo kuuma tene,
